preamble: In certain southwestern dialects of Asturian, a Romance language spoken in northern Spain, the voiced palatal lateral approximant /ʎ/ has undergone a notable phonetic transformation. This sound, often represented by the digraph 'ḷḷ' in these dialects, can be realized as a voiceless retroflex affricate /ʈ͡ʂ/, a distinctive feature known as 'che vaqueira'.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves a fortition process, changing from an approximant to an affricate

examples.note: The word 'lluna' (moon) in standard Asturian with /ʎ/ corresponds to 'ḷḷuna' with /ʈ͡ʂ/ in 'che vaqueira' dialects.
The word 'lleite' (milk) in standard Asturian with /ʎ/ corresponds to 'ḷḷeite' with /ʈ͡ʂ/ in 'che vaqueira' dialects.

sources: García, L. (2017). Asturian. Journal of the International Phonetic Association, 47(2), 247-254.
